mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower borrowing costs for corporate refinancing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns (accuracy < 50%) or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over individual series in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals stabilization of manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crunch periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index below 0.1% confirms deflationary pressures, compelling central banks to maintain ultra-accommodative policy and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Deflationary CPI prints are followed by negative equity returns in >58% of observations, or the signal fails to predict returns when FEDFUNDS is already at the zero lower bound.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly payroll losses exceeding 200,000 jobs and a rising unemployment rate create a compounding negative feedback loop on corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor deterioration signal yields positive forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below zero reflects entrenched economic contraction, which suppresses corporate revenue forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day returns in >62% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production increase exceeding 0.5% signals manufacturing sector resilience that outweighs consumer weakness, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases above 0.5% are followed by negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the threshold effect disappears when retail sales decline concurrently.
